codeReview.summary: Implementation provides a complete property graph knowledge base with SQLite persistence, typed nodes/edges, query interface, BFS path finding, cross-reference analysis, and graph traversal. All verification steps pass: cargo build --workspace succeeds and cargo test -p swell-memory -- knowledge_graph passes all 6 tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The KnowledgeGraph trait in swell-core/traits.rs only defines 5 core methods (add_node, add_edge, get_node, query_nodes, traverse), but the implementation adds 7 additional public methods (find_dependencies, find_dependents, find_path, get_cross_references, get_nodes_in_file, get_nodes_by_type, delete_node, clear_repository, get_stats). These extended methods are only available on SqliteKnowledgeGraph directly, not through the trait, limiting abstraction. However, this pattern is consistent with other swell-memory extensions (e.g., SqliteMemoryStore has reinforce, mark_stale beyond MemoryStore trait). No documentation gap since AGENTS.md doesn't specify trait extension policy.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: trait KnowledgeGraph has 5 methods; SqliteKnowledgeGraph impl block has 14 public methods total

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the Rust skill guidance correctly. The transcript shows iterative development with clippy feedback being addressed (unused mut variables, unnecessary .into() calls). No procedure deviations observed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker ran cargo clippy multiple times, fixed all warnings (unused mut, unnecessary .into() conversions)

summary: The knowledge-graph feature implements a complete property graph knowledge base with SQLite persistence, typed nodes/edges, query interface, BFS path finding between code elements, cross-reference analysis, and graph traversal. All expected behaviors from the feature description are implemented: graph database with nodes and edges, query interface for dependency lookups, path finding between code elements, and cross-reference analysis. Tests pass and clippy shows no warnings. No blocking issues identified.
